cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any month-end readout only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.
cost_sensitivity.scope: Value-free migration metadata only; no execution cost estimate is produced.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: Month-end/quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning.
data_assumptions.missing_label_candidates: No new label candidates are declared by this legacy card.

exclusion_rules: Derive the flag from the actual exchange trading calendar (last available trading session in the month/quarter), carried as known-ahead point-in-time metadata under the _require_metadata_known_as_of guard so a holiday-shifted last session is never inferred from future data.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: A calendar-derived month-end flag is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms used. Risk of NON-orthogonality is mainly redundancy with day_of_week / session position already present, not with the dead price-action factors; this must be checked.

Residualize forward outcomes against regime/vwap_session factors AND against the existing day_of_week feature before scoring the month-end conditioner, to confirm it is not a relabeling of an existing calendar feature.
Month-end/quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: Month-end / quarter-end sessions are deterministic from the exchange calendar and known ahead, so the conditioner carries little lookahead hazard. The only hazard is mislabeling the last trading session when holidays shift it; this must use the actual trading calendar, not a naive calendar-day rule.
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: low lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card
timestamp_assumptions.post_event_knowable_features: The legacy card declares no post-event knowable feature list.

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: On scheduled month-end (and quarter-end) sessions, we test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead month-end-session flag and time-to-RTH-close differs from ordinary sessions (a rebalancing-flow conditioning hypothesis). Falsifiable; not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Calendar-driven portfolio rebalancing and index-fund flows can concentrate into the month-end / quarter-end close; whether this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.
risks: Month-end / quarter-end sessions are deterministic from the exchange calendar and known ahead, so the conditioner carries little lookahead hazard. The only hazard is mislabeling the last trading session when holidays shift it; this must use the actual trading calendar, not a naive calendar-day rule.

A calendar-derived month-end flag is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms used. Risk of NON-orthogonality is mainly redundancy with day_of_week / session position already present, not with the dead price-action factors; this must be checked.
Month-end/quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning.

Falsifiable; not a profitability claim.
